Petition For Instructions
Off Calendar Jan-16-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gary Rothstein for Union Bank of California. Woodford Rowland for and with Kathleen Padula.

Mr. Rothstein reported that a lender had been found, but that it was an expensive option. Mr. Rowland argued that it made no sense to sell due to the market and that there would be a capital gains tax of $96,000.00 and $40,000.00 in costs if the property was sold. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR# 11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera.

Petition For Instructions
Off Calendar Dec-19-2007 Continued To Jan-16-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gary Rothstein for the Petitioner. Woodford Rowland for Fred Padula, Barbara Padula and Katherine Padula (Objectors). Kathleen Padula (Dauhgter) in Pro Per.

Mr. Rothstein stated that he just received the objection last night, that the bank had contacted a broker to get a loan, that capital gains was an issue, and that two of the condos may need to be incumbered. The Court questioned how repayment of the loan would be made. Mr. Rowland argued that the properties should not be sold, that selling would only incur an unnecessary tax liability, and stated that the payments would come out of the proceeds from the loan. The matter was continued to January 16, 2008 for status on securing a loan. If the matter is resolved, the order is to be submitted.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR #11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Lewis.
